description:
You are a Linux systems engineer tasked with hardening a couple of configuration files that live under /home/user/configs.  
Make the following three changes and then record a short summary log:

1. In /home/user/configs/ssh_config.yaml  
   • Change the value of PermitRootLogin from “yes” to “no”.  
   • Add a new key/value pair on its own line inside the same file:  

        PasswordAuthentication: no  

   The file is plain-text YAML and currently contains only three lines (comment, PermitRootLogin, MaxSessions).  
   Do not modify any other keys or spacing—only perform the two edits described above.

2. In /home/user/configs/service_limits.toml  
   • Change the value of max_open_files from 1024 to 4096.  
   Do not alter any other lines in the TOML file.

3. After both files have been edited, create a new directory /home/user/hardening (if it does not already exist) and inside it create the file /home/user/hardening/hardening_summary.log with exactly these three lines, in exactly this order and with no extra whitespace or blank lines:

ssh_config.yaml PermitRootLogin set to no  
ssh_config.yaml PasswordAuthentication set to no  
service_limits.toml max_open_files set to 4096  

The automated test will compare the final content of the two configuration files and the log file verbatim against the expected state, so be precise.  
Only the three changes above should appear in the diff; everything else must remain byte-for-byte identical.
truth:
Initial state before the task starts:

1. File: /home/user/configs/ssh_config.yaml  
   Contents (3 lines, including line breaks exactly as shown):
# OpenSSH configuration
PermitRootLogin: yes
MaxSessions: 10

2. File: /home/user/configs/service_limits.toml  
   Contents (3 lines):
# System service limits
max_open_files = 1024
max_user_processes = 512

3. The directory /home/user/hardening does not exist.

Expected final state after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/configs/ssh_config.yaml  
   Contents (now 4 lines):
# OpenSSH configuration
PermitRootLogin: no
PasswordAuthentication: no
MaxSessions: 10

2. /home/user/configs/service_limits.toml  
   Contents (3 lines, with only the numeric change):
# System service limits
max_open_files = 4096
max_user_processes = 512

3. /home/user/hardening/hardening_summary.log  
   Contents (exactly 3 lines, no trailing blank line):
ssh_config.yaml PermitRootLogin set to no
ssh_config.yaml PasswordAuthentication set to no
service_limits.toml max_open_files set to 4096
name: task_000000_af0637d0
